rationale: The citing paper’s core outcome is hedonic consumption (food and nonfood) across seven studies, and the cited paper is a seminal test of consumer choice between hedonic and utilitarian goods, directly matching that focal construct. Given this strong construct-level overlap and the exact DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.37.1.60.18718), the citation is plausibly foundational/theoretical rather than an obvious incidental mention.
